generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T TEREBINTHINA. Oil of Turpentine. Obtained from various species of conifers. The various oils of Turpentine differ in physical and chemical properties according to their different sources. French Turpentine is obtained from Pinus maritima, Venetian from Pinus larix, American from Pinus tæda. Preparation , Dilutions with pure alcohol. Authorities. ( 1 to 9 , from Hartlaub and Trink's Annals, 3, 118.) 1 , Seidel; 2 , Woost; 3 , Act. Wratisl., p. 365; 4 , Badnische Annalen der ges. Heilkunde, 2, 2, p. 67; 5 , Ermisch, in Hufel. Smith, Lond. Journ. of Med., April, 1850 (Am. Journ. of Med. Sci., 1850 (2), 158), a boy took 1/2 ounce of the oil; 22 , Frederick H. Johnson, Med. Times, new ser., vol. 3, 1851, p. 380, M. Rev., vol. 2, 1858, p. 312, Mr. W. took large doses of Turpentine to expel tapeworm; 29 , same, case of Miss ---, æt. fifteen, who was treated with large doses for tapeworm when she was eleven years old; 30 , Editor, ibid., an allopath gave a girl, æt. ten years, a large dose, and also used large quantities in injections, for ascarides; 31 , Prof. Hoppe, N. Z. für H. K., 7, 177, proving, by dropping on the left elbow; 32 , Purkinje, Hempel's Mat. (ibid.), a man drank about 1 1/2 ounces of the oil; 34 , Wibmer (ibid.), a man took the oil for tapeworm, 1 ounce in two doses, within an hour, in the morning, same dose in the evening (first day), 1 1/2 ounces in emulsion in space of an hour (second day); 35 , Dr. Buckner, A. H. Z., 68, 131, took 6th dil. on account of neuralgic pain in the left arm; 35 a , same, took 2 drops of 1/10th; 36 , Philip Miall, Lancet., 1869 (1), p. 360, a child, æt. fourteen weeks, swallowed an unknown quantity of the oil, death in fifteen hours; 37 , M. Benoit, La Presse Méd. Belge, February 20th, 1870 (Practitioner, vol. 4, 1870, 249), effects of small doses given for sciatica; 38 , Almen, Memorabilien, 1870 (N. Z. für H. K., 21, 40), effects on several patients who took by emulsions up to 100 drops; 39 , J. Warburton Begbie, M.D., Edin. Med. Journ., vol. 17, 1871, p. 39; 40 , Sir Thomas Watson, Lectures on Principles and Practice of Physic, vol. 1, p. 663 (ibid.); 41 , Dr. Andrew Duncan (Edinb. New Dispens., p. 553, ibid.); 42 , Weber, Monart. f. Ohreheik Jahrg., 5, No. 3, and Med.-Chir. Rundschan, 1872 (Practitioner, 9, 118), a girl, suffering with pain in neck and ear, took 3 capsules, each containing 15 drops, in morning and afternoon, 4 capsules in the evening; for some days following, successively smaller doses. MIND. ► Intense excitement of the nervous system, [15] . ► Anxiety, [18] ; (after one hour), [19] . ► Very distressing anxiety, with great weakness on going to bed (first day), [19] . ► Facility of thought, [17] . ► *Dulness of mind; inability to fix the attention long upon anything (after one hour), [19] . ► Slight stupefaction, [6] . ► Perfectly insensible (after three hours), [21] . ► *Comatose , [36] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Vertigo , [4] , [6] , [17] , [19] , [19a] . [10.] ► Vertigo, pressure, and fulness in the head, threatening apoplexy; after the lapse of three hours, the cerebral irritation abated in consequence of a spontaneous rejection of the oil (second day), [34] . ► Slight vertigo, [6] . ► Dizziness, with nausea and diminished appetite (second day), [2] . ► Momentary vertigo, even to falling down, whereby it became black before the eyes (after six, and fifty-eight hours), [2] . ► The patient was supposed to be dying, but was found to be intoxicated by a free dose of Turpentine, [40] . ► I have seen large doses produce temporary intoxication, and sometimes a kind of trance, lasting twenty-fours hours, without, however, any subsequent bad effects, [41] . ► The intoxicating effect of wine was very much heightened by oil of Turpentine, especially the vertigo, [33] . ► Soon seized with all the symptoms of intoxication; from evening till morning he lay drenched in sweat, in a state of stupor, from which he did not wake until next morning, very weak, and with his head in a state of bewilderment; when undertaking to stand still, he staggered about; he was relieved by a profuse discharge of urine, which had the odor of violets, [33] . ► General Head. ► Head dull, [6] . ► Fulness in the head (first evening), [34] . [20.] ► Extraordinary fulness and pressure in the head, so that the patient constantly cried out, "My head! my head!" and an attack of apoplexy was apprehended, [4] . ► *Headache , [6] , [13] , [13a] , [18] , [19] . ► General dull headache, with colic, lasting three days, [2] . ► Tearing, but not very violent, headache alternately till evening, at one time disappearing, at another returning, [2] . ► Slight tearing headache (after one hour), [2] . ► Suffered from headache until the next day, [27] . ► Violent pain in the head, [17] . ► Pressure in the whole head, [2] . ► Pressive headache in the whole head, with qualmishness, now disappearing, now returning, [2] . [30.] ► Heavy pressive headache over the left eye, lasting about half an hour, while sitting and during mental work, [2] . ► A stitch, as with a knife, in the forehead (after twelve hours), [2] . ► Tearing from the forehead out towards the right ear, which is very hot, while the left ear is cold to touch, [2] . ► A smarting-tickling pain in the left temporal region, coming and going, in the evening in bed, which disappears on rubbing (after sixty hours), [2] . ► A transient stitch in the mastoid process behind the right ear (after two hours), [2] . EYE. ► Eyes sunken, [25] . ► Eyes sunk and surrounded by a dark ring, [26] . ► Contracted pupils, [36] . ► Pupils violently contracted (after three hours), [22] . ► Black points and spots float for a moment before the eye, when walking in the open air, not when looking at a distance, with very transient sensation of vertigo (after two hours and a half), [2] . EAR. [40.] ► Ringing and singing in the ears, as from the ticking of a watch, increasing for four hours (after six days), [2] . NOSE. ► A peculiar kind of coryza, without disturbance of the general condition, and without the usual accompanying symptoms, and without any premonition, consisting of thin watery liquid, at times from one, at other times from both nostrils, lasting two days (after three to four days), [2] . ► *Violent nosebleed , [2] . FACE. ► Spasm of the face, beginning under the chin, affecting the face, even to the cheek-bones, with nervous drawings in the cheeks; these symptoms are felt by the prover, but there is no visible contraction of the face; these symptoms pass off, to be succeeded by a supraorbital headache, as if those parts were bruised, [24] . ► Countenance anxious, [25] . ► Countenance anxious, shrunk, and pale, [26] . ► *Countenance pallid (after three hours), [22] . ► *Face very pale (after one hour), [19] , [25] . ► Redness of the face, [18] . ► Drawing in the bones, in the right half of the face and in the forehead, in the evening (fourth day), [1] . [50.] ► Lips could scarcely be moved, [25] . MOUTH. ► Drawing in the teeth (first day), [1] . ► (The gum, which burnt, as if sore, nearly every morning, was loose, painful to pressure, and bled easily on brushing the teeth, became entirely well after a few days), (curative action), [2] . ► Burning in the tip of the tongue; the papillæ very prominent (first day), [1] . ► Breath and perspiration tainted with a Turpentine odor for upwards of a week, [21] . ► Breath cold, [25] . ► Phlyctænules in the mouth, [19a] . ► Some burning in the mouth while taking the drug, [6] . ► Sensation of dryness of all the mucous membranes, [18] . THROAT. ► Scraping in the throat frequently provokes hacking cough, in the evening (first day), [1] . [60.] ► Scraping sensation in the throat, [2] . ► Sensation of warmth and scraping in the pharynx and stomach, [18] . ► An agreeable coldness in the throat, immediately, [1] . ► No power over deglutition (after three hours), [22] . STOMACH. ► Appetite and Thirst. ► Hunger and thirst (after one hour and a half), [19] . ► Sudden violent desire for rue (to eat cut up on bread and butter), of which he had already eaten freely, in the evening, during vigorous and enjoyable mental work (after fifteen hours), [2] . ► She had less appetite than usual; food of which she most fond was not relished, and though she was very glad of it, yet she could eat very little, [2] . ► Loss of appetite, [18] ; for two days, [2] . ► *Anorexia , [29] . ► Very great thirst and appetite (after one hour), [19] . [70.] ► Thirst, [18] , [20] . ► Violent thirst, [11] . ► Eructations. ► Rancid eructations, [2] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea, [18] ; (first day), [19] . ► Nausea, yet inability to vomit, owing to excessive weakness, [26] . ► Meat nauseated her for more than a week, [2] . ► Slight transient nausea, in the morning, [2] . ► Inclination to vomit, [6] . ► *Vomiting , [4] , [11] , [18] , [19a] , [34] . ► Vomiting four or five times (after one hour), [8] . [80.] ► Vomited freely (after a draught of warm water and melted butter), [22] . ► *Vomiting of yellowish mucus , [6] . ► *Vomiting of mucus , [5] . ► Stomach. ► Loud rumbling in the stomach and intestines, immediately after dinner; the pain extended into the side, [2] . ► Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, so that slight touch on the abdomen, especially on the epigastric region, could not be endured (second and following days), [4] . ► The symptoms of gastro-enteritis made their appearance (second day), [36] . ► Heat in the stomach , [13] , [13a] , [19] . ► Burning in the stomach , [4] , [6] , [34] . ► Sensation between pain and burning in the pit of the stomach (after half an hour), [29] . ► Griping beneath the pit of the stomach, with nausea and eructations (after one hour), [1] . [90.] ► It seems as though he had swallowed a small ball, which remained in the pit of the stomach, [2] . ► Pressure in the pit of the stomach after eating, as if the food had been swallowed too hastily, [2] . ► Slight pressive pain in a small spot in the pit of the stomach, relieved by stooping, lying, and on deep breathing (after half an hour), [2] . ► Pressure in the stomach, [18] . ► Pressure as from without in the pit of the stomach, [2] . ► Pressure in the stomach becoming slight nausea, relieved by eructations, [2] . ► Pressure in the stomach, in the evening in bed (lying on the left side), which prevents his falling asleep, relieved and gradually removed after the emission of flatus, while lying on the right side (after seventeen hours), [2] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► Griping in the hypochondriac region, [6] . ► Pressive and cutting pain in the left hypochondriac region, while sitting, disappearing on motion, [2] . ► Violent burning pressure in the hypochondria, [2] . [100.] ► Burning in the right hypochondrium (after seventeen hours), [2] . ► Umbilicus and Sides. ► The umbilical region seems retracted, cold, just as though a cold, round plate were pressed against it, while lying down after dinner, [2] . ► Sticking and griping in the right side soon after an evacuation of the bowels, [2] . ► Slight pressure and drawing in the left side of the upper abdomen, [2] . ► Transient, jerking sticking in the left side of the upper abdomen, in the evening while urinating (after thirty-sixth hours), [2] . ► Tearing cutting in the left side of the abdomen, in the evening, in bed, when lying on the right side; afterwards also on the right side, [2] . ► General Abdomen. ► Abdomen very much distended , after eating food which he relished, [2] . ►► *Distension of the abdomen , as after a dose of salts , bitter water, or the like, [2] . ► Fulness in the abdomen, as if he had eaten too much, [2] . ►► *Feeling of distension in the abdomen, as from flatus , soon obliging him to rest (after three-quarters of an hour), [2] . [110.] ► Rumbling in the abdomen, [5] . ► Rumbling and gurgling in the abdomen, followed by colic in the evening, [2] . ► Rumbling and gurgling in the abdomen in the morning, [2] . ► Rumbling and griping in the abdomen, and thin stool, [6] . ►► *Meteorismus , [18] . ► Heaviness in the abdomen after the midday nap, [2] . ► Violent irritation of the bowels, [15] . ► *Colic (second day), [24] , [25] . ► Griping in the abdomen (after a quarter of an hour), [5] . ► Colic, with violent movements in the intestines, [18] . [120.] ► She complained of constant colic in the whole abdomen, extending thence into the legs, both during rest and motion, [2] . ► Violent pain in his bowels, but no discharges from them, [12] . ► Violent and continued abdominal pain, causing the patient to be doubled up, [26] . ► Sensation, as if the intestines were drawn back to the spinal column (after one hour), [19] . ► Pressure beneath the diaphragm, which extended from the left to the right side, after eating, [2] . ► Pressure in the abdomen, with a feeling of heaviness and emission of flatus, [2] . ► Feeling as though a violent purging would ensue, [2] . ► Slight cutting pain in the upper abdomen (after one hour and a half), [2] . ► Feeling of coldness in the abdomen as though uncovered and naked, after dinner, [2] . ► Hypogastrium and Iliac Regions. ► Cutting in the lower abdomen, [2] . [130.] ► Cutting in the lower abdomen, with distension of some parts, as if a hernia would protrude, [2] . ► Feeling as if the pubes were suddenly forced asunder, [2] . ► Transient tearing pain in the symphysis pubis, [2] . ► The inguinal glands are painful with slight swelling in the evening, while sitting (after thirty-six hours), [2] . ► Cutting drawing from the abdominal ring into the left testicle, disappearing while stooping, [2] . ► Constant more or less severe dragging and cutting in the right groin, [2] . ► Movement and working in the inguinal region, as though a hernia would protrude, especially while sitting with the legs outstretched, [2] . ► Drawing in the groins and thighs, [2] . ► Transient cutting drawing in the right groin, [2] . ► Cramp, dragging, and pressure from within outward in the groin, as though a hernia would protrude, now in the right, now in the left groin, disappearing while walking (after twenty-four, thirty-six, and sixty hours, and after three weeks), [2] . RECTUM AND ANUS. [140.] ► Burning in the anus, [5] . ► Violent burning and crawling in the anus, with a sensation as if worms were creeping out, [6] . ► Burning in the anus after stool, [6] . ► Slight burning in the anus while standing, [2] . ► Tickling and burning in the anus, [6] . ► Itching burning in the anus after a stool, as from blind hæmorrhoids, [2] . ► Urging to stool without result, [2] . ► Pressure to stool after eight hours, as though another evacuation would follow, [2] . ► In the evening he is obliged to go to stool for the fourth time, but without result, [2] . STOOL. ► Diarrha. ► Diarrha, [18] , [42] . [150.] ► Hypercatharsis, [15] . ► Quickly purged (after second dose), [13a] . ► Diarrha, six to eight times a day, with evacuation of half a vesselful of tapeworm, [8] . ► At five o'clock in the afternoon, a gentle purging evacuation, [13] . ► Evacuations thin, yellow, with discharge of tapeworm and round worms, [6] . ► Stool thin, greenish yellow, smelling of Turpentine, [6] . ► Several thin yellow stools, with discharge of tapeworm, [5] . ► Frequent soft stools, [19a] . ► A pasty evacuation, with colic and burning in the anus (first day), [1] . ► Fæces of a dark-chestnut brown, and unnaturally dry, [2] . [160.] ► Two stools in the evening (second day), [34] . ► Constipation. ► Evacuation of the bowels diminished, the stool was harder than usual, [2] . ► Stool diminished in quantity (instead of one evacuation he had two), [2] . ► Stool scanty, hard, [2] . ► Constipation with distended abdomen, [2] . ► Obstinate constipation for four days, [19] . ► The bowels remained inactive until he had taken eight ounces of the compound infusion of Senna, with ten grains of Calomel; the evacuations, when passed, were extremely fetid, black and slimy, but gave no smell of turpentine, [21] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Kidneys and Bladder. ► Symptoms of genuine urethritis, with extremely painful erections as in chordee, [18a] . ► *Heaviness and pains in the region of the kidneys (second day), [23] . ► *Pressure in the kidneys in the morning while sitting , disappearing on motion, [2] . [170.] ► *Transient drawing in the right kidney, extending thence into the right hip , [2] . ►► *Violent burning-drawing pains in the region of the kidneys (second to sixth day), [1] . ► Pain in the kidneys and diabetes, and died hydropical in twenty-five days, [10] . ► Pressive pain in the left kidney (after ten hours), [2] . ► Inflammation of the bladder supervened, [30] . ► Spasmodic pain in the bladder while sitting, frequently recurring, [2] . ► Violent twinging and cutting in the bladder, which alternates with a similar pain just above the navel, less when walking in the open air, worse during rest (after two days), [2] . ► A transient movement in the region of the bladder during a stool, as if the bladder were suddenly distended and bent forward, [2] . ► Burning in the bladder, and when urinating also in the urethra, [2] . ► Urethra. ► Burning during micturition, frequently very violent, [18a] . [180.] ► Burning in the urethra, [2] . ► Slight burning in the urethra while urinating (first day), [1] , [2] . ► Micturition and Urine. ► Inclined to urinate more frequently; each time the urine was scanty, [2] . ► Increased amount of urine (first day), [1] ; (second and third days), [2] , [19a] . ► Profuse discharge from the urinary organs, [21] . ► Diminished micturition, [6] . ► Urine diminished in quantity, [2] . ► She is now incontinent of urine , and will probably continue so to the end of her days, [30] . ► *Dysuria , [18] , [18a] , [19a] , [42] . ► Strangury, [19a] . [190.] ► *Strangury, bloody urine , and its total suppression, [11] . ► *Most distressing strangury, more violent than I had ever before witnessed, and attended with a greater loss of blood. A great degree of soreness remained in the bladder for several days , [12] . ► Complete suppression of urine, [3] . [Effects from use for stone.] ►► *Bloody urine , [16] , [19a] . ► Urine very scanty, red, sometimes bloody, or else copious and light colored, [18a] . ►► Urine scanty and bloody , and had a strong terebinthinate smell, [20] . ► Urine very scanty and red, or else, very copious and light colored, but in both circumstances smelling of violets, [18] . ► The urine smells strongly of violets (after half an hour), [6] , [19] , [23] . ► The urine has the so-called odor of violets in a high degree, the secretion is somewhat increased, [4] . ► *The urine has the peculiar odor of violets , but of the natural color, [2] . [200.] ► Voided a small quantity of urine, denoting the effects of the turpentine (soon); in the afternoon, the urine presented more powerfully the odor of turpentine, [12] . ► The urine is clear, but on standing becomes turbid, with a reddish-white sediment (second day), [1] . ► The urine deposits a thick, slimy, whitish-yellow sediment, has a wine color and smells strongly of violets, [9] . ► The urine deposits a slimy sediment (after twelve hours), [2] . ► Sugar in the urine, [38] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Violent spasmodic drawing in the left testicle and long the course of the left spermatic cord (after two hours and a half), [2] . ► Drawing in the left testicle while sitting (after three-quarters of an hour), [2] . ► Transient burning in the posterior portion of the right testicle, [2] . ► An emission at night (first day), [1] . ► Tearing in the mons veneris, [2] . [210.] ► Menstruation delayed for two days, and more scanty than usual, [2] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► The mucous membrane of the air-passages become dry, as in the first stage of catarrh, [18a] . ► Distressing prickling in the trachea, as in commencing bronchitis, [18a] . ► Voice. ► Voice gone, [25] , [26] . ► Cough and Expectoration. ► Cough, as from a foreign substance in the trachea, with a kind of cramp in the larynx, as in whooping cough; inspiration in six or seven catches; the cramp soon became so violent that he could scarcely inspire, followed by scraping in the throat (soon), [35] . ► (The short dry cough was aggravated, especially while lying down after eating), [1] . ► *Expectoration streaked with blood, [18a] . ► Respiration. ► Increase of the respiratory movements, [21] . ► Respiration hurried, short, and anxious, [26] . ► She complained of short breath, [2] . [220.] ► *Difficult respiration , [23] . ► Difficult respiration, the lungs seemed over-distended, [4] . ► Breathing labored (second day), [34] . ► Breathing stertorous (after three hours), [21] . CHEST. ► Auscultation disclosed mucous râles, and minute crepitation through both lungs, [34] . ► Spastic contraction of the muscles of the chest and neck, in the evening (first day), [19] . ► Sensation of soreness in the lower part of the chest, lasting a long time and very marked, [35a] . ► Pressive pain behind the sternum, [18a] . ► Burning in the chest, along the sternum, gradually extending over the whole chest, and disappearing with transient stitches at both nipples (after warm drinks); (after three minutes), [2] . ► Transient itching pain in the left pectoralis major muscle, [2] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Præcordia. [230.] ► Frightful oppression in the præcordial region (soon), [33] . ► A feeling of warmth at the heart while sitting, in the evening (of which she had noticed nothing during the whole day, though she had been in constant motion), so that she was obliged to yawn a great deal, with collection of water in the mouth (after fourteen hours), [2] . ► Heart's Action. ► Palpitations, [17] ; in the evening (first day), [19] . ► Acceleration of the circulation, [21] . ► Pulse. ► Hard rapid pulse, [18] , [18a] . ► Pulse rapid and weak (after three hours), [22] . ► Pulse and respiration quickened (in ten minutes), [13] . ► *Pulse quick, small, compressible, and almost imperceptible, but regular , [36] . ► *Pulse thready and scarcely perceptible , [26] . ► *Pulse almost imperceptible , [25] . [240.] ► Pulse rose from 65 to 86, [23] . ► Pulse 69 (before taking); fuller, 75 (after five minutes); small, hard 80 (after half an hour); small, hard, 82 (after one hour); small, irregular, 86 (after one hour and a half); small, weak, irregular, and intermittent, in the evening (after twelve hours), [19] . NECK AND BACK. ► Gradual painful drawing from the nape of the neck up into the occiput, which then extended to the forehead (after one hour), [2] . ► Severe pain in back, [20] . ► Pressive pain in the back, which extended up to between the shoulders, and there became a throbbing, [2] . ► Drawing pain in the back while sitting, especially in the evening, [2] . ► Drawing in the dorsal and lumbar muscles, with a sensation of heaviness and indolence on moving in the open air, [2] . ► She complains of drawing pain in the small of her back, [2] . ► Pain and increased warmth in the lumbar region, over the kidneys, and also in the hypogastric region; these places became sensitive to pressure, [18a] . EXTREMITIES. ► Heaviness in the limbs, [2] . [250.] ► Acute pain in all the joints, [26] . ► Soon after she partially lost the use of her right arm, and of her left leg, [29] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► She could only advance the arm from the body a short distance, [29] . ► But little use of the right arm, [29] . ► He had no control over his hand and arm when attempting to write, [26] . ► She could raise the shoulderblade and bend the arm in the elbow, but the hand fell with a jerk into the position in which she wished to place it, [29] . ► Pain as from a sprain in the muscles of the left upper arm (third day), [1] . ► Drawing in the bones of the upper arms in the morning (second and third days), [1] . ► Trembling of the hands (after one and a half hours), [19] . ► Fingers insensible and asleep in the evening (first day), [19] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. keynotes.text: Ter. was introduced to homœopathy by Hartlaub. A proving of it appeared in Hartlaub and Trinks' Annalen. Many symptoms have been added from poisonings and overdosings in old-school practice. According to Taylor, children are particularly sensitive to its action. Illness caused by sleeping in newly painted rooms is due in great part to the turpentine. "A stimulant and tonic to the mucous membranes, especially of the bladder and urethra: it proves useful in gleet, leucorrhœa, and cystorrhœa." This is Milnes' account of the old-school use of Ter. as an internal remedy. Brunton gives these points: (1) Applied to the skin Ter. acts as an irritant and rubefacient, causing a sensation of burning, and vesicles if applied for any length of time. (2) Inhaled it causes sneezing, tightness across eyes, and dyspnœa. (3) Given internally it causes burning in mouth and salivation; in stomach, sensation of heat or cold; gastro-enteritis, with vomiting and diarrhœa; ulceration of intestines. The pulse is sometimes slowed, sometimes quickened. Respiration is quickened and spasmodic; the drug is partly excreted by the lungs and acts on the mucous membrane, lessening its secretion. The temperature sometimes rises, sometimes falls. Voluntary movement is diminished; reflex action lowered; blood-pressure lowered, and vessels dilated. Diminishes the quantity of urine; gives the urine an odour of violets; causes lumbar pain, burning in urethra, painful micturition, hæmaturia. Among the uses of Ter., Brunton mentions the treatment of biliary colic. For this it is given in ether in the proportion of one part of the Oil of Turpentine to three of Ether. Homœopaths have confirmed the value of this. Throughout this list of effects burning is a constant note, and gives the key to many cases calling for Ter.─burning in gums, tongue, tip of tongue, mouth, throat, stomach, rectum, and anus, kidneys, bladder, and urethra, uterus, air passages, chest, and sternum. The last was particularly noticed after warm drinks, the pain running along the sternum with the drink and spreading in the chest. Burning extends from kidneys along ureters. Ter. is an irritative, sensitive remedy. Cooper has found it indicated in children (especially teething children) when they fly into passions. There is irritation of the meninges and often ascarides or other intestinal worms. In one case of maniacal fury in a man it did good. This irritability manifests itself in some cases of spasms and convulsions, and Lippe observed a case in point (H. P., x. 480). A woman had made her feet sore by walking, and applied turpentine to them. This was followed by a state like hydrophobia; she had spasms whenever she saw water or heard it poured, or saw a bright object; and also whenever she attempted to urinate. Ter. is a great hæmorrhagic and its hæmaturia is particularly characteristic: Smoky, turbid urine depositing a sediment like coffee-grounds. Dull pain, or burning pain in region of kidneys, burning during micturition are leading indications. Ter. corresponds to many cases of albuminuria and hæmaturia after scarlatina; and also to the consequent dropsy. This last may indicate Ter. in many affections of the genito-urinary sphere. "Burning in uterus" is very characteristic. Peritonitis, metritis, metrorrhagia will probably need Ter. if the burning is present. The hæmorrhages of Ter. are generally passive; dark, and fetid. Purpura hæmorrhagica calls for it if there are fresh ecchymoses in great numbers from day to day. The hæmorrhages may occur from any orifice. Additional keynotes of Ter., which will be decisive if combined with others, are: (1) Smooth, glossy, red tongue, as if deprived of papillæ. (2) Excessive tympanites. (3) Drowsiness. In capillary bronchitis, the child is drowsy. Stupor and great weakness are found in many Ter. conditions, rendering it appropriate in cases of typhus, typhoid, and uræmic poisoning. "Drowsy with retention of urine." With the tympanites there is generally extreme sensitiveness to touch. With cystitis and uro-genital and rectal troubles there is sensitiveness of hypogastrium and pains in symphysis pubis. Pains go from left to right across bowels and then shoot upward. From being exposed to turpentine odour, a lady, 35, got pain as from pinchings in the throat, with lifeless feeling in the tongue and dryness of the mouth, followed by pains in r. eyeball, which extended back to same side of occiput (Cooper). S. H. Roberts (B. M. J., Dec. 25, 1875) maintains Ter. has a specific effect in tonsillitis when applied externally. Marc Jousset gave Ter. 1x with marked benefit in two cases of bronchial asthma. He was led to give it in the first case by coincident hæmaturia; but this was not present in the second (L'Art Méd., April, 1901). A remedy may be known by its antidotes and antidotal properties. Ter. antidotes and is antidoted by Phos. A young man who was badly poisoned by a low attenuation of Phos. got more relief from Ter. 3x than from any other remedy. Brunton says it is only Ozonised Ter. that is effective here. The preparation I used was the ordinary one. Hering says the ozonised oil is recommended as a prophylactic in malarial and African fevers, a few drops being given daily on a lump of sugar. George Royal (Med. Cent., ix. 70) gives three cases illustrating the action of Ter.: (1) Typhoid, third week, in man 24. Extreme distension, rapid, small, compressible pulse, cold sweat in lower limbs. Ter. 6x two drops in water every half-hour. Oil of Ter. was applied locally to the abdomen mixed with lard, and later in the evening, when there was already slight improvement, an injection containing turpentine was administered. This relieved the patient of a large quantity of flatus, and next morning he was on the high-road to recovery. (2) Pyæmia after abortion at third month, brought on by patient herself. Enormous distension; lower limbs covered with cold sweat; lochia thin, scanty, offensive. Ter. given in another case brought about slow improvement for three days, when Chi. 30 was indicated and finished the cure. (3) Teamster, 36, had severe nephritis after being out in cold rain. Constant desire to urinate, severe pain in back, down ureter, to bladder; urine scanty, very high-coloured. Temperature 102°. Pulse weak, 130. Ter. 6x every half-hour; and hot fomentations on kidneys greatly relieved. After three days he was able to leave his bed. Royal considers cold sweat of lower limbs, and rapid pulse with the distension, as leading indications. A keynote of Burnett's for Ter. is: "Pains in the bowels which = frequent micturition." Peculiar Sensations are: As if he would pitch forward on walking As of a band round head. As if sand thrown violently in eye. As of a seashell sounding in l. ear. In ear as of striking of a clock. As from hasty swallowing in epigastrium. As if he had swallowed a bullet, which had lodged in pit of stomach. Sense of anxiety and utter prostration about epigastrium. Umbilical region as if covered with a round, cold plate. As if intestines were being drawn towards spine. As if abdomen distended with flatus. As if diarrhœa would set in. As if inguinal hernia would appear. As if symphysis pubis were suddenly forced asunder. As if foreign body had entered windpipe. As from electric shocks. Twitching of limbs. Crawling tingling as if parts were asleep. As if hot water running through a tube in nerve. Sprained pains, contusive pains, and rheumatic pains appear in the pathogenesis, and notably pressure and drawing pains. These show the suitability of Ter. to the effects of injury and rheumatic conditions. It is on this property that certain popular embrocations containing turpentine owe their reputation. Ter. is specially Suited to: Children (dentition, nose-bleed, worms). Bleeders. Nervous women (amenorrhœa; dysmenorrhœa; headache). Complaints of old people; of people of sedentary habits. Chronic rheumatism and gouty complaints. The symptoms are: By touch. By pressure (in region of bladder it = convulsions). Effects of falls and injuries. Lying on left side, > turning to right. Sitting . Stooping >. Motion >. (Pain in crest of ilium from motion and least jar.) Walking in open air . Night; 1-3 a.m. Damp dwellings . Damp cellar = diarrhœa. Damp weather = neuralgia in legs. (Ter. is hydrogenoid.) Cold water > burning in anus. > Belching and passing flatus. Loose stool > nausea.

A Text-Book of Materia Medica. (Characteristic, Analytical, and Comparative.) By Pr. Allen Corson Cowperthwaite, M. D., PH. D., LL. D. TEREBINTHINA . (Oleum Terebinthinae.) Common name.- Oil of Turpentine. General Analysis.- The chief action of turpentine is upon the kidneys, where it produces irritation, congestion, and inflammation, together with haematuria and albuminuria. It also affects to some extent all mucous surfaces, producing a tendency to congestion and catarrhal inflammation ; but more especially does it act upon the mucous lining of the bladder and urethra, giving rise to inflammation and strangury. Characteristic symptoms. Mind.- (Terebinthina) Stupefaction ; comatose condition (uraemia). (Bell.) Head.- (Terebinthina) Vertigo ; headache ; intense pressure and fullness of the head. Nose.- (Terebinthina) Violent nosebleed. (Acon., Bell., Bry., Ham.) Face.- (Terebinthina) Pale, earthy color of the face. (Ars.) Mouth.- (Terebinthina) Tongue red, smooth, and glossy, as if deprived of papillae. Stomach.- (Terebinthina) Loss of appetite. Vomiting of mucus. Burning in the stomach. Abdomen.- (Terebinthina) Excessive distention of the abdomen (Cinch., Graph., Hep. s .) ; meteorism ( Acon., Phos. ac .) ; colic. Stool.- (Terebinthina) Stools of mucus and water ; worse in the morning. Intestinal catarrh and diarrhoea, with nephritis. Urinary Organs.- (Terebinthina) Heaviness and pressure in region of kidneys. Violent, burning, drawing pains in region of kidneys. Distressing strangury, followed by soreness. (Canth.) Urine scanty and bloody. ( Ars ., Canth., Colch., Ham.) Urine having the odor of violets. Respiratory Organs.- (Terebinthina) Difficult respiration, as if from congestion of the lungs. Great dryness of the mucous membranes of the air passages. Pulse.- (Terebinthina) Quick, small, thready, almost imperceptible. Generalities.- (Terebinthina) Great prostration. (Ars., Cinch., Phos.) Occasional subsultus. (Hyos., Stram.) Cold, clammy perspiration all over the body. (Camph., Tabac ., Verat. alb.) Therapeutic Range.- (Terebinthina) Nephritis ; Bright's disease ; haematuria ; cystitis ; urethritis ; gonorrhoea ; intestinal haemorrhages and ulcerations ; worms ; diarrhoea ; dropsy ; emaciation. Compare.- (Terebinthina) Canth., Copab. Antidote.- (Terebinthina) Phos. Copyright © Robert Séror 2003
